snapshot: At 11:00 JST the Nikkei was 53,697.19, up 1,444.91, while Prime-market breadth stood at 1,491 gainers, 73 losers and 15 unchanged.
The Nikkei 225 night-session futures close was 53,030 and TOPIX futures ended at 3,607, leaving cash equities with some catch-up room versus overnight trade.
USD/JPY was around 158.60 at 11:12 JST. The pullback from the 159s has eased part of the earlier 'bad yen' pressure.
Crude fell sharply on ceasefire headlines, with WTI back in the high $80s during Asian trade. That is the main support behind today's relief move.
Still, the morning high has already faded, so the afternoon is more about catalyst-driven stock selection than simple index chasing.

drivers.driver: Ceasefire headlines and softer front-end oil
drivers.why_today: Reports that Washington sent Tehran a 15-point peace plan pushed WTI down into the high $80s. That sets up a futures-led relief rally in Tokyo.
USD/JPY retraced from the 159s to the 158.60 area. That modestly reduces import-cost pressure and gives domestic-facing names a little breathing room.

drivers.driver: After-close IR, MBO and capital-policy news
drivers.why_today: Capital is concentrating in names with clear disclosures, including Solasto, note, Senshu Ikeda Holdings and Stella Chemifa.

headline_inventory.headline: The Nikkei 225 night-session futures close was 53,030 and TOPIX futures ended at 3,607
Reuters' New York market summary showed USD/JPY at 158.98, the U.S. 10-year around 4.33%, and another decline in U.S. equities on March 24
Oil futures dropped sharply on ceasefire speculation, with WTI back in the high $80s and Brent near the $100 line
Reuters expects the Nikkei to extend gains on March 25, with a projected 52,500-53,100 range
At 11:00 JST the Nikkei was 53,697.19 (+1,444.91), while Prime-market breadth stood at 1,491 gainers, 73 losers and 15 unchanged
note extended gains on its capital-and-business alliance with KADOKAWA and the planned third-party allotment
Solasto announced an MBO and recommended tendering into the offer
Senshu Ikeda Holdings revised up both earnings guidance and its year-end dividend forecast
Stella Chemifa raised full-year guidance and added a 110th-anniversary dividend
Niterra revised up both its full-year net-profit outlook and dividend forecast
Tokio Marine announced a strategic partnership with Berkshire's insurance group, including a 2.49% stake sale and a simultaneous buyback
Nippon Sheet Glass said the privatization report is broadly factual and that its board was scheduled to decide on the matter

watchlist.name: Tokio Marine Holdings
watchlist.why_today: A Berkshire insurance unit is taking a stake, and the company paired the deal with a buyback. Even at 11:00 JST it was still trading near record territory.
watchpoints: Can it stay near the high-level indication into the afternoon
Does the market reward the buyback offset more than the dilution concern
Does buying stay concentrated in Tokio Marine rather than the insurance sector broadly
falsifiers: The market focuses first on dilution from the treasury-share disposal
The opening premium fades as headline freshness wears off
watchlist.red_team: As a mega-cap, the stock can lose momentum quickly after a strong opening gap.

watchlist.name: note
watchlist.why_today: It announced a capital-and-business alliance with KADOKAWA and a third-party allotment. At 11:00 JST the stock was still up more than 11%, showing that synergy hopes were beating dilution concerns.
watchpoints: Does the cooperation story outweigh dilution concerns
Can real turnover stay alive into the afternoon
falsifiers: The market focuses first on dilution from the new share issuance
The earnings impact is judged too distant
watchlist.red_team: The partnership angle is strong, but the equity issuance can destabilize the initial reaction.

watchlist.name: Solasto
watchlist.why_today: The MBO and the 1,119-yen tender-offer price are now the key reference points. This remains a high-priority event name for afternoon monitoring.
watchpoints: Does the stock keep closing the gap to the event price
Does turnover stay alive even after the first match
Can it avoid a sharp fade once the opening premium is reflected
falsifiers: Investors turn more cautious on the detailed tender conditions
Trading dries up quickly once the initial spread compression is done
watchlist.red_team: Once the tender spread is mostly closed, the upside can become mechanically capped.

watchlist.name: Niterra
watchlist.why_today: It lifted full-year net profit and the annual dividend. Even after yesterday's first reaction, the stock was still positive at 11:00 JST.
watchpoints: Are both the earnings revision and the dividend hike rewarded cleanly
Can the stock-specific story beat a generic export rebound
falsifiers: The market reduces the move to a simple FX-driven revision
Pure index-beta rebound trading drowns out the stock-specific catalyst
watchlist.red_team: A meaningful part of the upgrade still leans on external conditions, especially FX.

watchlist.name: Senshu Ikeda Holdings
watchlist.why_today: It raised both earnings guidance and the year-end dividend. At 11:00 JST the stock was up more than 7%, adding a fresh numeric catalyst to the regional-bank theme.
watchpoints: Can it maintain relative strength inside the regional-bank group
Does the higher payout keep attracting early buying
Can volume stay alive after the opening print
falsifiers: The stock is swallowed by a generic regional-bank move
The prior reaction already priced in most of the news
watchlist.red_team: Its stock-specific edge can fade quickly if the bank theme turns too broad.

watchlist.name: Stella Chemifa
watchlist.why_today: It moved to an operating-profit growth plan and added a 110th-anniversary dividend. The stock is still holding gains on day two.
watchpoints: Does the market value the earnings improvement more than the anniversary-dividend angle
Can turnover stay active into the afternoon
falsifiers: The move is treated as a one-off dividend trade
Weakness in chemicals spills over and caps follow-through

deep_dive.title: Tokio Marine: the key question is whether the buyback-offset logic can outlive the Buffett headline

lines: This is not just a generic Buffett-linked headline. The more important point is how the market reads the combination of treasury-share disposal and the offsetting buyback.
As a liquid insurance mega-cap, Tokio Marine can attract both index-rebound money and stock-specific capital at the same time.

After the March 23 close, the company disclosed a strategic partnership with a Berkshire insurance subsidiary and a treasury-share disposal, while also approving a buyback intended to offset dilution.
Even by 11:00 JST on March 25 the stock was still trading near record territory, which makes this a second-day confirmation setup rather than a one-print headline trade.
Does the market reward the buyback offset more than it worries about dilution from the disposal.
Does turnover remain stock-specific rather than simply spreading across the insurance sector.
Dilution concerns dominate the first read and overwhelm the buyback logic.
The stock cannot sustain the opening premium and slips into a simple sector-sympathy trade.
If the broader rebound fades after the open, a mega-cap like Tokio Marine can still face quick profit-taking.
The cooperation path may take time to become tangible, leaving the stock exposed to headline fatigue.

no_trade_conditions: If the Nikkei gives back most of the 11:00 JST gain and cannot stay meaningfully positive
If WTI decisively reclaims the low-$90 area and the morning relief tone is undone by a fresh oil rebound
If USD/JPY snaps back toward the upper 159s or intervention-style volatility suddenly returns
If fresh catalyst names such as Tokio Marine and note fail to hold their early premiums and the market falls back into a simple index rebound only
